memoryArtifacts.headline: The Garretts -- Before America
memoryArtifacts.description:
The Garretts — Before America
by John E. Garrett
Last year, I published my work entitled, "1000 Years of Garrett Family History". The emphasis in that effort
was the genealogy of the Garretts since their immigration to America in the mid-seventeenth century. This
work examines how the Garretts evolved during the generations that they spent in England, Wales and
Ireland from the time the first ancestor landed in England until the first Garrett came to America.
My research involved the use of:
 A commercial genealogy website named Ancestry.com.
 Extensive use of the Google search engine on the Internet
The first 100 years, starting in the eleventh century, was the most interesting. That was where I found a
remarkable relationship between my ancestors and King Edward the Confessor, King William the Conqueror
and King Henry I. My first ancestor to land in England, Otho Geraldini, grew-up with Edward before he
became King; his son Walter FitzOtho was in William, the Bastard's army; and his son Gerald grew-up with
Prince Henry (King William"s son) who became King Henry I. Their close relationship is demonstrated by the
vast amount of land, castles and titles bestowed upon them.
In retrospect, I am impressed at how important the genes of the spouses are to the family's genealogy. The
best example of this is when my ancestor, Gerald of Windsor married Nesta Rhys, a Welsh Princess. There
offsprings were all outstanding. The next "high" came when the Garrett's became merchants in the 16th
century, opened trade with Russia, became Lord Mayor of London and honorary knighthood.
To make this work more readable, I have placed several interesting "asides" in the Appendix. For example,
the de Clares, of Richard "Strongbow" de Clare fame married into the Garretts. I found extensive genealogy
on the ancestry of Richard "Strongbow" de Clare going back before 100 AD. So, I put his data into two
appendices. One for the French ancestors and another for the Viking ancestors.

By Mike Winder

zh.lifeSummary: 當William FitzGerald於大約1106年出生在英國威爾斯布洛可爾卡維時，他的父親Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle33歲，母親Nesta ferch Rhys23歲。他與Maria de Montgomery結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1173年在威爾士布洛可爾卡維享年69歲，埋葬在威爾士。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William FitzGerald于大约1106年出生在英国威尔斯布洛可爾卡維时，他的父亲Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle33岁，母亲Nesta ferch Rhys23岁。他与Maria de Montgomery结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1173年在威尔士布洛可爾卡維享年69岁，埋葬在威尔士。

nameMeanings.name: FitzGerald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Jarrell
meanings: English (Kent and Middlesex; of Norman origin): variant of Jarrold, itself a variant of Gerald .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William FitzGerald was born about 1106, in Carew, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle, was 33 and his mother, Nesta ferch Rhys, was 23. He married Maria de Montgomery. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1173, in Carew, Pembrokeshire, Wales, at the age of 69, and was buried in Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William FitzGerald est né environ 1106, à Carew, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Nesta ferch Rhys, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Maria de Montgomery. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1173, à Carew, Pembrokeshire, Galles, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als William FitzGerald um 1106 in Carew, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle 33 und seine Mutter Nesta ferch Rhys 23. Er hat Maria de Montgomery geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1173 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Carew, Pembrokeshire, Wales und wurde in Wales begraben.

Lui sposato Maria de Montgomery. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1173, nel Carew, Pembrokeshire, Galles, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: William FitzGeraldが約1106年にイギリスウェールズペンブロケシュアイアカーエウで生まれた時，父Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castleは33歳，母Nesta ferch Rhysは23歳であった。Maria de Montgomeryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1173年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアカーエウで69歳で死去し，ウェールズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William FitzGerald(이)가 대략 1106년 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르 카레에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Nesta ferch Rhys(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Maria de Montgomery(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1173년 웨일스 p브로케실르 카레에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William FitzGerald nasceu aproximadamente 1106, em Carew, Pembrokeshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Nesta ferch Rhys, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Maria de Montgomery. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1173, em Carew, Pembrokeshire, Gales, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William FitzGerald родился около 1106 г. в местности карю, пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Nesta ferch Rhys, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria de Montgomery. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1173 г. в местности карю, пемброкешир, Уэльс в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности Уэльс.

Se casó con Maria de Montgomery. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1173, en Carew, Pembrokeshire, Gales, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en Gales.
